description:
Measure the background sky level of a galaxy image, subtract it cleanly, and extract a spatial profile.

Instructions:
1. Open `~/AstroImages/raw/uit_galaxy_sample.fits` in AstroImageJ.
2. Convert the image to 32-bit float (`Image > Type > 32-bit`) to prevent data clipping during subtraction.
3. Draw a rectangular Region of Interest (ROI) in an empty area of the sky (no stars/galaxy).
4. Measure the statistics (`Analyze > Measure`) and save the results table as a CSV to `~/AstroImages/measurements/background_measure.csv`.
5. Note the 'Mean' value from the measurement.
6. Subtract this exact Mean value from the entire image using `Process > Math > Subtract...`. (Do NOT use the 'Subtract Background' filter).
7. Save the resulting subtracted FITS image to `~/AstroImages/processed/uit_galaxy_bg_subtracted.fits`.
8. Select the Line tool and draw a straight line that passes completely through the bright central core of the galaxy.
9. Generate a spatial profile plot (`Analyze > Plot Profile`).
10. Save the profile data to `~/AstroImages/measurements/galaxy_profile.csv`.
